title: Sync & Backup
lead: Cachly can sync your data across your devices and back it up to the cloud, so a new phone or a reinstall doesn’t mean starting over.

sections.heading: iCloud sync
sections.text: With iCloud sync enabled, Cachly keeps your data in step across the iPhone and iPad signed into the same Apple ID — so lists, settings and more follow you from device to device.

sections.heading: Backups & restore
sections.text: Cachly can create backups of your data to the cloud. The Backups screen lists your backups so you can restore one — invaluable when moving to a new device or recovering after a reinstall. You can browse the underlying iCloud documents from the iCloud files screen.

sections.heading: Backing up the database file
sections.text: For a full manual copy, the database file Cachly.sqlite holds all your offline lists and pending logs (but not settings). Grab it two ways: Files app: Browse › On My iPhone › Cachly , long-press Cachly.sqlite and Share (e.g. AirDrop to a computer). Finder / iTunes File Sharing: connect the device, select it, choose File Sharing › Cachly , select Cachly.sqlite and Save. To restore , add your saved Cachly.sqlite back via File Sharing (Add), confirming the overwrite. 💾 Make a backup before a big change — like a major offline-map migration or clearing space — so you can roll back if needed.

sections.heading: Dropbox & Google Drive
sections.text: Cachly also integrates with Dropbox and Google Drive , letting you move files (such as GPX) to and from those services for import/export workflows. See Import & Export .
